sections.paragraph:
τρώγω (Hom. et al.; TestJob 12:2; SIG 1171, 9; PGM 7, 177; Sb 5730, 5. Not found in LXX, EpArist, Philo or Joseph. B-D-F §101 s.v. ἐσθίειν; 169, 2; Rob. 351; JHaussleiter, Archiv für lat. Lexikographie 9, 1896, 300–302; GKilpatrick in: Studies and Documents 29, ’67, 153) to bite or chew food, eat (audibly), of animals (Hom. et al. ‘chew, nibble, munch’) B 10:3.—Of human beings (Hdt. et al. and so in Mod. Gk.) τὶ someth. (Hdt. 1, 71, 3 σῦκα; Aristoph., Equ. 1077) B 7:8. ὁ τρώγων μου τὸν ἄρτον as a symbol of close comradeship (Polyb. 31, 23, 9 δύο τρώγομεν ἀδελφοί) J 13:18 (s. Ps 40:10 ὁ ἐσθίων ἄρτους μου, which is the basis for this pass.). W. gen. (Athen. 8, 334b τῶν σύκων) Hs 5, 3, 7. Abs. B 10:2. W. πίνειν (Demosth. 19, 197; Plut., Mor. 613b; 716e) Mt 24:38. J uses it to offset any tendencies to ‘spiritualize’ the concept so that nothing physical remains in it, in what many hold to be the language of the Lord’s Supper ὁ τρώγων τοῦτον τὸν ἄρτον 6:58. ὁ τρώγων με vs. 57. ὁ τρώγων μου τὴν σάρκα (w. πίνων μου τὸ αἷμα) vss. 54, 56.—B. 327. DELG. M-M. TW.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Mod. Gk. Mod. Gk. = Modern Greek

someth. someth. = something

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

gen. gen. = genitive

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1019
